---
title: "Klima Protocol — Fee vs. Principal"
subtitle: "Revenue and risk for a carbon-retirement intermediary"
---

```{=html}
<style>
  /* Drawable custom-curve widget — landing page only. */
  .pc-curve-box { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa; padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem; margin: 0.75rem 0; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem; margin: 0 0 0.6rem; align-items: center; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button { padding: 0.3rem 0.7rem; border: 1px solid #888; background: #fff; color: #111; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 500; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button:hover { background: #eef; border-color: #555; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar .hint { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #666; margin-left: auto; }
  .pc-curve-canvas { display: block; max-width: 100%; height: auto; touch-action: none; cursor: crosshair; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; }
</style>
```

A carbon-retirement intermediary retires carbon credits for its
clients by buying kVCM tokens on the open market and burning them.
Two pricing choices frame the business: pass the token cost through
and add a **fee** (no balance-sheet exposure), or quote a **fixed USD
price** up front and absorb the token risk on the **principal** book.
The two choices produce different revenue distributions, and once the
principal book is chosen the shape of its loss tail is an active
design decision.

## Three findings

The [Model](model.qmd) page derives each claim in full; what follows
is the shape-of-the-answer.

### Fee and b2b share a kernel but not a sign

Fix $Q$ at the break-even level $Q^*$ and the fee and b2b operating
books have identical expected P&L. Their distributions are near
mirror images, though: both are linear in the shared random kernel
$I_T = \int_0^T S_t \, dt$, but fee enters with a plus (revenue,
bounded below by zero) and b2b with a minus (upside capped at $Q
N$, downside unbounded). Matching means does not match distributions
— at $Q^*$ the b2b book has paid for a loss tail the fee book does
not carry.

### The treasury cancels b2b's $I_T$ term when fully matched

With retirement flow $\lambda$ tonnes per unit time and total tonnes
$N = \lambda T$, buying the full $N P$ tokens at $t = 0$ gives a
treasury whose consumption integral contributes $+ P \lambda I_T$ to
desk P&L — exactly opposite to b2b's $- P \lambda I_T$. The sum
collapses to the deterministic $N(Q - P S_0)$. Partial pre-purchase
$(k, C_{\mathrm{basis}})$ — inventory notional $k$ in kVCM and sunk
USD basis $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ — leaves a tail $J_\alpha =
\int_{\alpha T}^T S_t \, dt$ uncancelled, where the coverage fraction
$\alpha = \min(1, k / (N P))$ is the share of retirements the
treasury can source from inventory; the desk SD shrinks with the
length of the covered window $[0, \alpha T]$, and $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$
translates the distribution without changing its shape. Quota-share
syndication of the b2b operating book is a second, multiplicative
dial: ceding a fraction $\beta$ to a counterparty against an up-front
premium collapses variance as $(1 - \beta)^2$, and at the actuarially
fair premium (counterparty risk load $\theta = 0$) cession preserves
expected P&L. Risk managers should track coverage and cession
separately from the unrealised P&L on held tokens.

### A barrier truncates the tail rather than rescaling it

Pre-buying and syndication multiply the loss tail by a constant.
Switching pricing to the fee book when the token crosses the upper
barrier $H = h \cdot S_0$ (with barrier multiple $h \ge 1$) caps every
post-barrier retirement at non-negative fee revenue, so the tail is
bounded rather than scaled. Under the default calibration, $h = 1.25$
collapses $\mathrm{CVaR}_{95}$ — the mean loss in the worst 5 % of
paths — from ≈ \$3,900 to ≈ \$49 ($\approx 80\times$) while *raising*
expected P&L. The three levers — capital (via the treasury),
counterparty credit ($\beta$), market timing ($h$) — compose
multiplicatively and consume distinct resources; together they close
the asymmetry the first finding identifies. Every symbol above is
defined in the [Model page's setup and notation
section](model.qmd#setup-and-notation).

---

## Testing a specific trajectory

The findings above describe *averages* over all GBM futures.
Scenarios that pin down a specific narrative — *token halves at
mid-year*, *replay the last twelve months of kVCM* — read more
naturally off a single realised path. Draw one below (click and
drag); every book's deterministic P&L recomputes against the
comparable Monte Carlo mean in real time. Toolbar presets cover the
common shapes, including a historical-replay when the kVCM series has
been fetched.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## How the drawn curve enters the books

Your samples define a base path. The noise slider sets
$\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} \ge 0$, the annualised variance of an
additive log-normal shock layered on top of the base curve (a
robustness dial distinct from the GBM volatility $\sigma$ that drives
the comparison Monte Carlo); $\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} > 0$ activates
the overlay, seeded by the PRNG slider for reproducibility. From the
realised path we compute $I_T$, the coverage-uncovered tail integral
$J_\alpha = \int_{\alpha T}^T S_t \, dt$, the switching split $I_\tau
= \int_0^\tau S_t \, dt$ and $J_\tau = \int_\tau^T S_t \, dt$ — where
$\tau$ is the first time the realised path crosses $H = h \cdot S_0$,
or $T$ if it never does — all by trapezoid rule, and $S_T$ as the
last sample. These are the same quantities every book on the
[Simulator](simulator.qmd) consumes. Leave noise at zero to read
deterministic P&L off the curve; raise it to see how robust a
narrative is to typical day-to-day jitter.
:::

### Presets

Each button installs a starting shape; refine by hand after clicking.

- **Flat @ $S_0$** — constant path. Under $\mu = 0$ this is the GBM
  mean, so the three book P&Ls should land near their MC means.
- **Linear ±50%** — calibrates how drift moves each book.
- **Crash @ ½T** — mid-year collapse; informative when coverage falls
  just inside the uncovered window.
- **Spike @ ⅓T** — brief rally the back-to-back book has to source
  through.
- **Random walk** — a hand-drawn GBM approximation, reference only.
- **Historical** — the real kVCM series from `data/kvcm-historical.json`
  when populated by `npm run fetch:prices`; disabled otherwise.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
historicalPrices = {
  // Fetched at build time by `npm run fetch:prices` (Alchemy Prices API).
  // Missing or empty file ⇒ the "Historical" preset below is inert.
  try {
    const r = await fetch("data/kvcm-historical.json");
    if (!r.ok) return { data: [] };
    return await r.json();
  } catch (e) {
    return { data: [] };
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// "Today" anchor for the custom-curve x-axis. Prefers the fetchedAt
// timestamp baked into kvcm-historical.json by `npm run fetch:prices`
// so the rightmost tick reflects build time rather than page-open
// time; falls back to the current date if the build-time fetch is
// missing. Captured once per page load so ticks don't shift while the
// document is open.
buildDate = {
  const iso = historicalPrices && historicalPrices.fetchedAt;
  if (iso) {
    const parsed = new Date(iso);
    if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) return parsed;
  }
  return new Date();
}
```

::: {.side-panel-left .side-panel .pc-controls}
### Inputs

Inventory, contract, horizon, and the price process (drift, variance,
and optional Merton jumps) driving the comparison Monte Carlo. Jumps
are off by default — Poisson jump intensity $\lambda_J = 0$ jumps per
year; see the [Simulator](simulator.qmd) for a wider-ranging sweep.

### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, value: 125, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof P = Inputs.range([100, 25000], { step: 10, value: 1790, label: "P (kVCM / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mu = Inputs.range([-1, 1], { step: 0.005, value: 0.05, label: "drift μ (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigma2 = Inputs.range([0, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.25, label: "variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

### Jumps (Merton)

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaJ = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 0, label: "jump intensity λ_J (/yr)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof muJ = Inputs.range([-0.5, 0.5], { step: 0.01, value: -0.05, label: "mean log-jump μ_J" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigmaJ = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.35, label: "log-jump SD σ_J" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, value: 21000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Step = 1 (not 5): a coarser step causes a range-input snap when the
// value is set to `history.length - 1`, inflating nPoints past the
// available history and leaving a non-historical fill at the left edge.
viewof Tdays = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const fallback = 365;
  const dflt = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : fallback;
  return Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 1, value: dflt, label: "horizon T (days)" });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, value: 180, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Syndication

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof beta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "cession β" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof theta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "counterparty load θ" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof premiumMode = Inputs.radio(["sharpe", "cvar"], { value: "sharpe", label: "premium mode" })
```

### Switching

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof barrierRatio = Inputs.range([1, 3], { step: 0.01, value: 3, label: "switching barrier h = H/S₀ (3 ≈ no switch)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostLocked = Inputs.toggle({ label: "lock f_post to f", value: true })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostSlider = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "post-switch fee rate f_post" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// null when locked (simulateSwitching interprets this as "use inputs.fee");
// a numeric value when the user wants an independent post-switch rate.
feePost = feePostLocked ? null : feePostSlider
```

### Curve settings

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveYMax = Inputs.range([0.01, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.2, label: "canvas y-max (USD / kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveVar = Inputs.range([0, 2.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0, label: "noise variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
findingsResetButton = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const defaultTdays = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : 365;
  const defaults = {
    pi0: 125, P: 1790,
    mu: 0.05, sigma2: 0.25,
    lambdaJ: 0, muJ: -0.05, sigmaJ: 0.35,
    lambdaDay: 1, kPre: 300000, cBasis: 21000,
    Tdays: defaultTdays, Q: 180, fee: 0.4,
    beta: 0, theta: 0, premiumMode: "sharpe",
    barrierRatio: 3, feePostLocked: true, feePostSlider: 0.4,
    curveYMax: 0.2, curveVar: 0, seed: 42,
  };
  const views = {
    pi0: viewof pi0, P: viewof P,
    mu: viewof mu, sigma2: viewof sigma2,
    lambdaJ: viewof lambdaJ, muJ: viewof muJ, sigmaJ: viewof sigmaJ,
    lambdaDay: viewof lambdaDay, kPre: viewof kPre, cBasis: viewof cBasis,
    Tdays: viewof Tdays, Q: viewof Q, fee: viewof fee,
    beta: viewof beta, theta: viewof theta, premiumMode: viewof premiumMode,
    barrierRatio: viewof barrierRatio, feePostLocked: viewof feePostLocked,
    feePostSlider: viewof feePostSlider,
    curveYMax: viewof curveYMax, curveVar: viewof curveVar, seed: viewof seed,
  };
  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.className = "pc-reset";
  const btn = document.createElement("button");
  btn.type = "button";
  btn.textContent = "Reset to defaults";
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    for (const k of Object.keys(defaults)) {
      const el = views[k];
      el.value = defaults[k];
      el.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
    }
  });
  wrap.appendChild(btn);
  return wrap;
}
```

:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
import {
  mulberry32, xTicksAnchoredRight, formatTickDate, formatTickCurrency,
  simulate, simulateSwitching, summarise, samplePath,
} from "./lib/compiled/src/core/index.js"
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const S0 = P > 0 ? pi0 / P : 0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  const sigma = Math.sqrt(sigma2);
  return { S0, T, lambda, N, sigma };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Self-contained comparison MC for the drawn-curve table. Drift,
// variance, and jump parameters come from the sidebar; nPaths / nSteps
// stay pinned at the Simulator's defaults.
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    beta, premiumLoad: theta, premiumMode,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed,
  };
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  const customDeskSamples = new Float64Array(raw.b2bSamples.length);
  for (let i = 0; i < customDeskSamples.length; i++) {
    customDeskSamples[i] = raw.b2bSamples[i] + raw.treasurySamples[i];
  }
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    customDeskSamples,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    retained: summarise(raw.retainedSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
    treasury: summarise(raw.treasurySamples),
    customDesk: summarise(customDeskSamples),
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Switching-book MC sibling. Shares `seed` with `run` so the GBM path draws
// coincide bit-for-bit. The switching book is a pure operating book —
// matched-desk composition (switching + treasury) is deferred to Validation.
switchRun = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee,
    barrierRatio, feePost,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed,
  };
  const raw = simulateSwitching(inputs);
  return { inputs, ...raw, summary: summarise(raw.pnlSamples) };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof customCurve = {
  // One sample per day. The rightmost point is the build-time anchor
  // (values[nPoints-1] = today) and the leftmost is (Tdays) days ago,
  // so moving the horizon slider extends or trims the left edge
  // without reshaping the already-drawn right edge.
  const TDaysVal = Tdays;
  const nPoints = TDaysVal + 1;
  const width = 820;
  const height = 320;
  const margin = { top: 10, right: 12, bottom: 32, left: 58 };
  const plotW = width - margin.left - margin.right;
  const plotH = height - margin.top - margin.bottom;
  const yMax = curveYMax;
  const S0Val = derived.S0;
  const history = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const sigmaN = Math.sqrt(curveVar);
  const dt = derived.T / (nPoints - 1);
  const seedVal = seed;
  const muVal = mu;
  const lambdaJVal = lambdaJ;
  const muJVal = muJ;
  const sigmaJVal = sigmaJ;

  // Persist the drawn curve indexed by days-ago (0 = build-time anchor)
  // so shrinking and regrowing the horizon restores the previously
  // drawn samples at the leftmost part of the curve. NaN marks "never
  // drawn" so π₀/P slider changes still refresh the initial fill (via
  // the derived S₀) for days that haven't been touched.
  const MAX_TDAYS = 1095;
  const masterKey = "__findingsCustomCurveByDaysAgo";
  let master = window[masterKey];
  if (!(master instanceof Float64Array) || master.length !== MAX_TDAYS + 1) {
    master = new Float64Array(MAX_TDAYS + 1);
    master.fill(NaN);
    window[masterKey] = master;
  }

  // "random" means the Random path preset is live: a slider nudge
  // regenerates the path + cloud from the current params. Anything
  // else (drawn curves, other presets, Historical) stays put when
  // the jump sliders move. Persisted in window so the flag survives
  // cell re-runs.
  const modeKey = "__findingsCustomCurveMode";
  if (typeof window[modeKey] !== "string") window[modeKey] = "custom";

  // Seed the canvas with the historical series on first page load. The
  // one-shot window flag is required: without it, every slider-driven
  // re-run of this cell would overwrite user edits.
  const seededKey = "__findingsCustomCurveSeeded";
  if (!window[seededKey]) {
    window[seededKey] = true;
    if (history.length >= 2) {
      const fillCount = Math.min(history.length, MAX_TDAYS + 1);
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo < fillCount; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[history.length - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
    }
  }

  const values = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
    const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
    const stored = master[daysAgo];
    values[i] = isNaN(stored) ? S0Val : stored;
  }

  // Sample cloud built the same way as the simulator page: one
  // mulberry32(seed) stream, samplePath consumed sequentially
  // (mirrors src/core/simulate-run.ts:81, 103-104), so identical
  // (seed, μ, σ, λ_J, μ_J, σ_J, T, S₀) produce identical clouds on
  // both pages. Path 0 is the one the Random path preset promotes to
  // the drawn (orange) curve — same convention as the simulator's
  // highlighted first path.
  const N_CLOUD = 20;
  const cloudRng = mulberry32(seedVal);
  const cloud = new Array(N_CLOUD);
  for (let j = 0; j < N_CLOUD; j++) {
    const { S } = samplePath(cloudRng, {
      S0: S0Val,
      mu: muVal,
      sigma: derived.sigma,
      T: derived.T,
      nSteps: nPoints - 1,
      lambdaJ: lambdaJVal, muJ: muJVal, sigmaJ: sigmaJVal,
    });
    cloud[j] = S;
  }

  if (window[modeKey] === "random") {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = cloud[0][i];
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) master[nPoints - 1 - i] = values[i];
  }

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.className = "pc-curve-box";

  const toolbar = document.createElement("div");
  toolbar.className = "pc-curve-toolbar";
  const hint = document.createElement("span");
  hint.className = "hint";
  hint.textContent = "Click and drag on the canvas to draw a price path.";

  const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
  canvas.className = "pc-curve-canvas";
  canvas.width = width;
  canvas.height = height;
  const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");

  const indexToX = (i) => margin.left + (i / (nPoints - 1)) * plotW;
  const priceToY = (p) => margin.top + (1 - p / yMax) * plotH;
  const xToIndex = (x) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(nPoints - 1, Math.round(((x - margin.left) / plotW) * (nPoints - 1))));
  const yToPrice = (y) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(yMax, yMax - ((y - margin.top) / plotH) * yMax));

  const realised = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  function refreshRealised() {
    if (sigmaN === 0) {
      for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) realised[k] = values[k];
      return;
    }
    const rng = mulberry32(seedVal);
    let logNoise = 0;
    realised[0] = values[0];
    for (let k = 1; k < nPoints; k++) {
      const z = rng.normal();
      logNoise += sigmaN * Math.sqrt(dt) * z - 0.5 * sigmaN * sigmaN * dt;
      realised[k] = values[k] * Math.exp(logNoise);
    }
  }

  const DAY_MS = 86400000;
  const anchor = buildDate;
  const dateForDay = (d) => new Date(anchor.getTime() - (TDaysVal - d) * DAY_MS);
  const xTicks = xTicksAnchoredRight(TDaysVal);
  function redraw() {
    refreshRealised();
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, width, height);
    ctx.strokeStyle = "#e5e5e5";
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let g = 1; g < 5; g++) {
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, y);
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, y);
    }
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      if (d <= 0 || d >= TDaysVal) continue;
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      ctx.moveTo(x, margin.top);
      ctx.lineTo(x, margin.top + plotH);
    }
    ctx.stroke();

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#333";
    ctx.strokeRect(margin.left, margin.top, plotW, plotH);

    ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
    ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
    ctx.textAlign = "right";
    ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
    for (let g = 0; g <= 5; g++) {
      const priceVal = yMax * (1 - g / 5);
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.fillText("$" + priceVal.toFixed(3), margin.left - 6, y);
    }
    ctx.textBaseline = "top";
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      const isRight = d === TDaysVal;
      const isLeft = d === 0;
      ctx.textAlign = isRight ? "right" : isLeft ? "left" : "center";
      ctx.fillText(formatTickDate(dateForDay(d), TDaysVal), x, margin.top + plotH + 6);
    }

    ctx.font = "12px sans-serif";
    ctx.save();
    ctx.translate(14, margin.top + plotH / 2);
    ctx.rotate(-Math.PI / 2);
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("S_t (USD / kVCM)", 0, 0);
    ctx.restore();
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("date", margin.left + plotW / 2, height - 2);

    if (S0Val > 0 && S0Val <= yMax) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.setLineDash([4, 3]);
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "bottom";
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.fillText("S₀", margin.left + 4, priceToY(S0Val) - 2);
    }

    const showCloud = lambdaJVal > 0 || derived.sigma > 0;
    if (showCloud) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#888";
      ctx.globalAlpha = 0.35;
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.lineJoin = "round";
      for (let j = 0; j < cloud.length; j++) {
        const S = cloud[j];
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, S[0]))));
        for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) {
          ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, S[i]))));
        }
        ctx.stroke();
      }
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
    }

    if (sigmaN > 0) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#4c78a8";
      ctx.globalAlpha = 0.7;
      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
      ctx.lineJoin = "round";
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, realised[0]))));
      for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) {
        ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, realised[i]))));
      }
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
    }

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#c94";
    ctx.lineWidth = 2;
    ctx.lineJoin = "round";
    ctx.beginPath();
    ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(values[0]));
    for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(values[i]));
    ctx.stroke();

    if (sigmaN > 0 || showCloud) {
      const rows = [];
      rows.push({ color: "#c94", width: 2, alpha: 1, label: "drawn (base)" });
      if (sigmaN > 0) {
        rows.push({ color: "#4c78a8", width: 1.5, alpha: 0.7, label: "realised (+ noise)" });
      }
      if (showCloud) {
        rows.push({ color: "#888", width: 1, alpha: 0.35, label: "sampled paths" });
      }
      const boxH = 6 + rows.length * 16;
      const lx = margin.left + plotW - 140;
      const ly = margin.top + 8;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.85)";
      ctx.fillRect(lx - 6, ly - 2, 140, boxH);
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#ccc";
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.strokeRect(lx - 6, ly - 2, 140, boxH);
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
      for (let r = 0; r < rows.length; r++) {
        const row = rows[r];
        const y = ly + 5 + r * 16;
        ctx.strokeStyle = row.color;
        ctx.lineWidth = row.width;
        ctx.globalAlpha = row.alpha;
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.moveTo(lx, y);
        ctx.lineTo(lx + 18, y);
        ctx.stroke();
        ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
        ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
        ctx.fillText(row.label, lx + 22, y);
      }
    }
  }

  function emit() {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      master[nPoints - 1 - i] = values[i];
    }
    container.value = {
      values: Float64Array.from(values),
      realised: Float64Array.from(realised),
      nPoints,
    };
    container.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
  }

  let lastIdx = null;
  let drawing = false;

  function apply(ev) {
    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = ((ev.clientX - rect.left) * canvas.width) / rect.width;
    const y = ((ev.clientY - rect.top) * canvas.height) / rect.height;
    const idx = xToIndex(x);
    const price = yToPrice(y);
    if (lastIdx === null || lastIdx === idx) {
      values[idx] = price;
    } else {
      const lo = Math.min(idx, lastIdx);
      const hi = Math.max(idx, lastIdx);
      const loP = lastIdx <= idx ? values[lastIdx] : price;
      const hiP = lastIdx <= idx ? price : values[lastIdx];
      for (let k = lo; k <= hi; k++) {
        const frac = (k - lo) / (hi - lo);
        values[k] = loP * (1 - frac) + hiP * frac;
      }
    }
    lastIdx = idx;
  }

  canvas.addEventListener("pointerdown", (ev) => {
    window[modeKey] = "custom";
    ev.preventDefault();
    drawing = true;
    lastIdx = null;
    canvas.setPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId);
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  canvas.addEventListener("pointermove", (ev) => {
    if (!drawing) return;
    ev.preventDefault();
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const endStroke = (ev) => {
    drawing = false;
    lastIdx = null;
    if (canvas.hasPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId)) canvas.releasePointerCapture(ev.pointerId);
  };
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerup", endStroke);
  canvas.addEventListener("pointercancel", endStroke);

  function setPreset(fn) {
    window[modeKey] = "custom";
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = fn(i / (nPoints - 1));
    redraw();
    emit();
  }
  function addButton(label, fn) {
    const b = document.createElement("button");
    b.type = "button";
    b.textContent = label;
    b.addEventListener("click", fn);
    toolbar.appendChild(b);
  }
  addButton("Flat @ S₀", () => setPreset(() => S0Val));
  addButton("Linear +50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 + 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Linear −50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 - 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Crash @ ½T", () =>
    setPreset((u) => (u < 0.5 ? S0Val : S0Val * Math.max(0.05, 1 - 1.6 * (u - 0.5))))
  );
  addButton("Spike @ ⅓T", () => {
    const peak = S0Val * 2.5;
    setPreset((u) => {
      const d = u - 1 / 3;
      return S0Val + (peak - S0Val) * Math.exp(-80 * d * d);
    });
  });
  addButton("Random path", () => {
    // Set the mode before the seed dispatch: the triggered cell re-run
    // reads modeKey during init and regenerates the cloud + path 0
    // from the new seed. Keeps a single regeneration codepath.
    window[modeKey] = "random";
    const newSeed = 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 9999);
    const seedView = viewof seed;
    seedView.value = newSeed;
    seedView.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
  });
  const historicalBtn = document.createElement("button");
  historicalBtn.type = "button";
  historicalBtn.textContent = history.length >= 2 ? "Historical" : "Historical (unavailable)";
  historicalBtn.disabled = history.length < 2;
  historicalBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    // Flag must be set before the Tdays dispatch so the re-run picks
    // it up and skips random-path regeneration.
    window[modeKey] = "custom";
    // Right-align the daily historical series: the most recent sample
    // lands on today (rightmost point) and earlier samples fill back
    // in toward the left.
    const dayCount = history.length;
    const targetTdays = Math.max(30, Math.min(MAX_TDAYS, dayCount - 1));
    const tdaysView = viewof Tdays;

    if (Number(tdaysView.value) !== targetTdays) {
      // Stash the series into master so the cell's re-run — triggered
      // by the Tdays change below — picks it up without a second fill.
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo <= targetTdays; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
      tdaysView.value = targetTdays;
      tdaysView.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
      return;
    }

    // Idempotent re-click: Tdays already at target, so redraw in place.
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
      values[i] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
    }
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  toolbar.appendChild(historicalBtn);
  toolbar.appendChild(hint);

  container.appendChild(toolbar);
  container.appendChild(canvas);

  redraw();
  container.value = {
    values: Float64Array.from(values),
    realised: Float64Array.from(realised),
    nPoints,
  };
  return container;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customScenario = {
  const { values: base, realised, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const T = derived.T;
  const lam = derived.lambda;
  const N = derived.N;
  const dt = T / (nPoints - 1);

  let IT = 0;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === 0 || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    IT += w * realised[k];
  }
  IT *= dt;

  const tauFrac = (lam > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lam * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * (nPoints - 1));
  const tailStartIdx = tailStartRaw >= nPoints - 1 ? nPoints : tailStartRaw;
  let tailInt = 0;
  for (let k = tailStartIdx; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === tailStartIdx || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    tailInt += w * realised[k];
  }
  tailInt *= dt;
  const consumptionInt = IT - tailInt;

  const ST = realised[nPoints - 1];
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lam * T * P);
  const feeRev = fee * P * lam * IT;
  const b2bOp = Q * N - P * lam * IT;
  const treasury = P * lam * consumptionInt + tokensLeftover * ST - cBasis;
  const customDesk = b2bOp + treasury;

  // Retained operating book: (1 − β) · b2b + premium. The premium scalar comes
  // from the MC run (run.premium) — derived from the run's b2b sample moments.
  const retainedOp = (1 - beta) * b2bOp + run.premium;

  // Switching — walk the realised path, bucket trapezoid pieces into I_τ / J_τ.
  // Barrier convention matches src/core/simulate-switching.ts: the step that
  // completes the crossing belongs to I_τ, τ = k·dt; h ≤ 1 fires at t=0.
  const H = barrierRatio * derived.S0;
  let switched = barrierRatio <= 1;
  let tauTime = switched ? 0 : T;
  let crossed = switched;
  let ITau = 0;
  let JTau = 0;
  for (let k = 1; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const piece = 0.5 * (realised[k - 1] + realised[k]) * dt;
    if (!switched && realised[k] >= H) {
      tauTime = k * dt;
      switched = true;
      crossed = true;
      ITau += piece;
    } else if (!switched) {
      ITau += piece;
    } else {
      JTau += piece;
    }
  }
  const fPost = feePost ?? fee;
  const switchingOp = Q * lam * tauTime - P * lam * ITau + fPost * P * lam * JTau;

  let maxS = -Infinity, minS = Infinity;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    if (realised[k] > maxS) maxS = realised[k];
    if (realised[k] < minS) minS = realised[k];
  }
  const meanS = IT / T;

  return {
    base, realised, nPoints, IT, tailInt, ST, tauFrac, tokensLeftover,
    feeRev, b2bOp, treasury, customDesk, retainedOp, switchingOp,
    crossed, tauTime,
    maxS, minS, meanS,
  };
}
```

_Orange: the base curve you drew. Blue (shown only when
$\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} > 0$): the noisy realised path fed into the
integrals, overlaid. Cycle the PRNG seed slider for different noise
realisations on the same base._

### Carbon price on the drawn path

The same drawn/realised path rescaled to USD per tonne
($\pi_t = S_t \cdot P$) — the unit the contract quotes in. Today $P$ is
a constant slider, so the curve shape matches the canvas; the cell is
written as $S_t \cdot P(t)$ so a future variable-$P$ conversion drops in
without changing the chart.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
carbonPriceRecords = {
  const { values, realised, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const sigmaN = Math.sqrt(curveVar);
  const hasNoise = sigmaN > 0;
  const hasCloud = (lambdaJ > 0) || (derived.sigma > 0);
  const dtDays = Tdays / (nPoints - 1);
  // Local conversion. Today P is a constant slider, so price-per-tonne
  // is a straight rescale of S_t. Written as a helper so a future
  // time-varying P_k drops in without touching the plot.
  const toTonne = (s) => s * P;

  const baseRows = new Array(nPoints);
  const realisedRows = hasNoise ? new Array(nPoints) : [];
  for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
    baseRows[i] = { tDays: i * dtDays, price: toTonne(values[i]), id: "base" };
    if (hasNoise) {
      realisedRows[i] = { tDays: i * dtDays, price: toTonne(realised[i]), id: "realised" };
    }
  }

  // Rebuild the 20-path cloud the same way as the canvas cell
  // (viewof customCurve, above) so the chart's grey cloud is bit-identical
  // to the canvas overlay under the same (seed, μ, σ, λ_J, μ_J, σ_J).
  const cloudRows = [];
  if (hasCloud) {
    const N_CLOUD = 20;
    const cloudRng = mulberry32(seed);
    for (let j = 0; j < N_CLOUD; j++) {
      const { S } = samplePath(cloudRng, {
        S0: derived.S0,
        mu,
        sigma: derived.sigma,
        T: derived.T,
        nSteps: nPoints - 1,
        lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
      });
      const id = "cloud-" + j;
      for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
        cloudRows.push({ tDays: i * dtDays, price: toTonne(S[i]), id });
      }
    }
  }

  return { baseRows, realisedRows, cloudRows, hasNoise, hasCloud };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 820,
  height: 320,
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: {
    label: "date",
    domain: [0, Tdays],
    ticks: xTicksAnchoredRight(Tdays),
    tickFormat: d => formatTickDate(
      new Date(buildDate.getTime() - (Tdays - d) * 86400000), Tdays),
  },
  y: { label: "carbon price (USD / tonne)", zero: true, tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    ...(carbonPriceRecords.hasCloud
      ? [Plot.line(carbonPriceRecords.cloudRows,
          { x: "tDays", y: "price", z: "id", stroke: "#888", strokeOpacity: 0.25 })]
      : []),
    Plot.line(carbonPriceRecords.baseRows,
      { x: "tDays", y: "price", stroke: "#c94", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    ...(carbonPriceRecords.hasNoise
      ? [Plot.line(carbonPriceRecords.realisedRows,
          { x: "tDays", y: "price", stroke: "#36c", strokeWidth: 1.5, strokeOpacity: 0.9 })]
      : []),
    Plot.ruleX([run.tauFrac * Tdays],
      { stroke: "#2a7", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ],
})
```

_Orange: drawn base rescaled by $P$. Blue: noise-realised path (when
$\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} > 0$). Grey: twenty sampled GBM trajectories
(when $\sigma > 0$ or $\lambda_J > 0$). Green dashed rule:
inventory-exhaustion time $\alpha T$._

### P&L over time on the drawn path

Each book's deterministic P&L accrued up to time $t$ along the
drawn/realised path. The rightmost point of each series reconciles with
the `P&L on drawn curve` column of the table below. The custom book
shows the characteristic kink at $\alpha T$ (inventory exhaustion); the
switching book (when $h < 3$) shows a second kink at $\tau$, the moment
the barrier is first crossed.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlOverTime = {
  const { realised, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const T = derived.T;
  const lam = derived.lambda;
  const dt = T / (nPoints - 1);
  const dtDays = Tdays / (nPoints - 1);
  const N = derived.N;

  // Keep the αT breakpoint identical to customScenario so the terminal custom
  // desk matches the table's `customDesk` to FP noise.
  const tauFrac = (lam > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lam * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * (nPoints - 1));
  const tailStartIdx = tailStartRaw >= nPoints - 1 ? nPoints : tailStartRaw;

  const H = barrierRatio * derived.S0;
  const includeSwitch = isFinite(barrierRatio) && barrierRatio < 3;
  const switchLabel = `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})`;
  const fPost = feePost ?? fee;

  // Cumulative state updated in lockstep with k.
  let I = 0;
  let ITau = 0;
  let JTau = 0;
  let switched = barrierRatio <= 1;
  let tauTime = switched ? 0 : T;

  // Treasury opens at MTM at t = 0; operating books all start at 0.
  const treasuryOpen = kPre * derived.S0 - cBasis;

  const rows = [];
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    if (k > 0) {
      const piece = 0.5 * (realised[k - 1] + realised[k]) * dt;
      I += piece;
      if (!switched && realised[k] >= H) {
        tauTime = k * dt;
        switched = true;
        ITau += piece;
      } else if (!switched) {
        ITau += piece;
      } else {
        JTau += piece;
      }
    }

    // Edge-weighted tail integral on [tailStartIdx, k]. Zero-width
    // (k === tailStartIdx) collapses to 0. O(nPoints²) overall but
    // nPoints ≤ 1096, so fine.
    let tailIk = 0;
    if (k > tailStartIdx && tailStartIdx < nPoints) {
      for (let j = tailStartIdx; j <= k; j++) {
        const w = (j === tailStartIdx || j === k) ? 0.5 : 1;
        tailIk += w * realised[j];
      }
      tailIk *= dt;
    }

    const tK = k * dt;
    const heldTokens = Math.max(0, kPre - lam * P * tK);
    const consumptionIk = I - tailIk;
    const feePnl = fee * P * lam * I;
    const b2bPnl = Q * lam * tK - P * lam * I;
    const retainedPnl = (1 - beta) * b2bPnl + run.premium * (tK / T);
    const treasuryPnl = P * lam * consumptionIk + heldTokens * realised[k] - cBasis;
    const customDeskPnl = b2bPnl + treasuryPnl;

    const tDays = k * dtDays;
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "fee", pnl: feePnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "b2b", pnl: b2bPnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", pnl: retainedPnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "treasury", pnl: treasuryPnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", pnl: customDeskPnl });
    if (includeSwitch) {
      const switchPnl = switched
        ? Q * lam * tauTime - P * lam * ITau + fPost * P * lam * JTau
        : Q * lam * tK - P * lam * I;
      rows.push({ tDays, book: switchLabel, pnl: switchPnl });
    }
  }

  return { rows, includeSwitch, switchLabel, treasuryOpen };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 820,
  height: 360,
  marginLeft: 80,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: {
    label: "date",
    domain: [0, Tdays],
    ticks: xTicksAnchoredRight(Tdays),
    tickFormat: d => formatTickDate(
      new Date(buildDate.getTime() - (Tdays - d) * 86400000), Tdays),
  },
  y: { label: "partial P&L (USD)", tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: [
      "fee",
      "b2b",
      "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)",
      "treasury",
      "custom desk = b2b + treasury",
      ...(pnlOverTime.includeSwitch ? [pnlOverTime.switchLabel] : []),
    ],
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.ruleX([run.tauFrac * Tdays],
      { stroke: "#2a7", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.line(pnlOverTime.rows,
      { x: "tDays", y: "pnl", stroke: "book", z: "book", strokeWidth: 1.75 }),
  ],
})
```

_Each line traces one book's deterministic cumulative P&L along the drawn
(or noise-realised) path. Operating books start at 0; the treasury opens at
its MTM $k \cdot S_0 - C_{\mathrm{basis}}$. Green dashed rule:
inventory-exhaustion time $\alpha T$. The switching series appears only
when the barrier $h < 3$._

### Deterministic P&L on the drawn path

The cards below report one-point statistics of the realised curve
(min/max, its mean $I_T / T$, terminal $S_T$, the coverage-uncovered
tail integral). The table then gives each book's P&L on that curve
next to its MC mean; the diff column is the headline stress-test
number. The switching row appears only when the barrier $h$ is below 3.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="derived-stats">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">min / max drawn S</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.minS.toFixed(4)} / ${"$" + customScenario.maxS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">mean drawn S (I_T / T)</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.meanS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">terminal S_T</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.ST.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tail integral ∫_{αT}^{T} S_t dt</div><div class="value">${customScenario.tailInt.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customRows = [
  { book: "fee",                          pnl: customScenario.feeRev,     mcMean: run.fee.mean,        diff: customScenario.feeRev     - run.fee.mean },
  { book: "b2b",                          pnl: customScenario.b2bOp,      mcMean: run.b2b.mean,        diff: customScenario.b2bOp      - run.b2b.mean },
  { book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", pnl: customScenario.retainedOp, mcMean: run.retained.mean,   diff: customScenario.retainedOp - run.retained.mean },
  { book: "treasury",                     pnl: customScenario.treasury,   mcMean: run.treasury.mean,   diff: customScenario.treasury   - run.treasury.mean },
  { book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", pnl: customScenario.customDesk, mcMean: run.customDesk.mean, diff: customScenario.customDesk - run.customDesk.mean },
  // Switching row only when the barrier is active, matching simulator.qmd.
  ...(isFinite(barrierRatio) && barrierRatio < 3
    ? [{ book: `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})`,
         pnl: customScenario.switchingOp, mcMean: switchRun.summary.mean,
         diff: customScenario.switchingOp - switchRun.summary.mean }]
    : []),
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(customRows, {
  columns: ["book", "pnl", "mcMean", "diff"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    pnl: "P&L on drawn curve",
    mcMean: "E[Π] (MC)",
    diff: "drawn − MC mean",
  },
  format: {
    pnl: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    mcMean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    diff: x => (x >= 0 ? "+$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
  },
})
```

_**drawn**: deterministic P&L implied by the sketched trajectory.
**MC mean**: $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$ from a self-contained Monte Carlo at
fixed defaults ($\mu = 0.05$, $\sigma^2 = 0.25$, no jumps, 5,000
paths). **diff** = drawn − MC mean: how this scenario deviates from
the average GBM future._

**Reading the diff column.** A negative diff on `custom desk` is the
stress-test signal — the token was pricier over the uncovered
window $[\alpha T, T]$ than GBM expects on average. Diffs near zero
mean the drawn curve is a typical GBM path, and at full coverage
($k = NP$, $C_{\mathrm{basis}} = NP \cdot S_0$) the custom desk
collapses to the deterministic matched total and is insensitive to
the curve by construction. The retained row applies the $(1 - \beta)$
cession dial to the b2b operating book and adds the MC-priced
premium; the switching row is only meaningful when the drawn path
crosses $H = h \cdot S_0$ before $T$ and is hidden above $h = 3$.

## Scope

GBM baseline with an optional compensated Merton jump overlay;
retirement demand deterministic. Gas, on-chain slippage, discounting,
and credit sit outside the model — the [Model page's limitations
table](model.qmd#limitations-and-next-steps) is authoritative.
Outputs are illustrative, not forecasts.
